skill.name: bitbucket-automation
skill.description: Automate Bitbucket repositories, pull requests, branches, issues, and workspace management via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Bitbucket workflows through Rube MCP integration for repositories, pull requests, branches, and issues
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 271 static findings are false positives from Markdown code formatting. The file is documentation-only with no executable code. External command detections are backticks around tool names, not actual execution. The single network reference is a legitimate MCP endpoint URL. Skill safely provides Bitbucket automation workflows through Rube MCP server.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: 271 'external_commands' detections are Markdown backticks formatting tool names (e.g., `BITBUCKET_LIST_WORKSPACES`), not actual code execution. The file is documentation-only SKILL.md with no executable content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading file - backticks wrap tool names in Markdown documentation, no executable code present

content.value_statement: Eliminate manual Bitbucket tasks with intelligent automation. Create pull requests, manage branches, track issues, and administer workspaces through natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage pull requests with reviewers and diff analysis
List and create branches in Bitbucket repositories
Create, update, and comment on issues with priority tracking
Search and filter repositories using BBQL query language
Add inline code review comments to pull requests
Manage workspace members and repository permissions
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Bitbucket OAuth
Reads tool schemas dynamically - tool availability may change
Cannot execute code directly on Bitbucket infrastructure

use_cases.title: Create Pull Requests with Reviewers
use_cases.description: Developers can create pull requests from feature branches to main, assign reviewers by UUID, and set proper destination branches without navigating the web interface.

use_cases.title: Track and Manage Issues
use_cases.description: Project managers can create bug reports with priorities, assign issues to team members, update issue states, and add comments for issue resolution tracking.

use_cases.title: Audit Repository Access
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers can list workspace members, filter repositories by project or privacy settings, and verify branch structures for compliance auditing.

prompt_templates.title: List Open Pull Requests
prompt_templates.prompt: List all open pull requests in the {workspace} repository named {repo_slug}. Show PR title, author, and creation date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants to see what code is currently under review

prompt_templates.title: Create Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request in {workspace}/{repo_slug} from {feature_branch} to {main_branch}. Title it '{title}' and assign these reviewers: {reviewer_list}.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer completes feature work and requests code review

prompt_templates.title: Create Bug Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a critical bug issue in {workspace}/{repo_slug} titled '{bug_title}'. Describe it as '{description}' and assign to {assignee_username}.
prompt_templates.scenario: QA tester discovers a critical production bug

prompt_templates.title: Review PR Code Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the pull request diff for PR {pr_id} in {workspace}/{repo_slug}. Limit to 50000 characters and show which files changed most.
prompt_templates.scenario: Code reviewer needs to analyze changes before approving

output_examples.input: List all open pull requests in my-workspace/frontend-repo
output_examples.output:
Found 3 open PRs:
- PR #42: Add user authentication (from feature/auth to main) by @alice
- PR #45: Fix navigation bug (from bugfix/nav to main) by @bob
- PR #48: Update dependencies (from chore/deps to main) by @charlie

output_examples.input: Create a branch from commit abc123 in my-workspace/backend-repo named feature/new-api
output_examples.output: Branch 'feature/new-api' created successfully from commit abc123def456... in repository 'backend-repo'. The branch is now available for development.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify available tool schemas before executing workflows
Use BBQL filters with double quotes around string values: name~"api" instead of name~api
Confirm with users before destructive operations like BITBUCKET_DELETE_REPOSITORY or BITBUCKET_DELETE_ISSUE
Set max_chars parameter on GET_PULL_REQUEST_DIFF to prevent large diffs from overwhelming context
anti_patterns: Do not omit destination_branch in PR creation if the repo main branch is not named 'main'
Do not use usernames for issue updates - UPDATE_ISSUE requires assignee_account_id UUID not assignee username
Do not include refs/heads/ prefix when creating branches - use 'feature/name' not 'refs/heads/feature/name'
Do not pull full pagination without checking response metadata - verify total count to avoid excessive API calls

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and why do I need it?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server that provides Bitbucket API integration. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server, then connect your Bitbucket account through OAuth to enable automation.

faq.question: Why does the skill say to search tools first?
faq.answer: Tool schemas may change as Rube MCP updates. Calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS ensures you have the current parameter names and required fields for each Bitbucket operation.

faq.question: What is BBQL and how do I use it?
faq.answer: BBQL (Bitbucket Query Language) filters list results. Use double quotes for strings: name~"api" finds names containing 'api'. Combine with AND/OR: name~"api" AND is_private=true.

faq.question: How do I assign reviewers to a pull request?
faq.answer: Use BITBUCKET_LIST_WORKSPACE_MEMBERS to get UUIDs, then pass them as objects: [{"uuid": "{123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000}"}]. UUIDs must include curly braces.

faq.question: Can I undo delete operations?
faq.answer: No. BITBUCKET_DELETE_REPOSITORY and BITBUCKET_DELETE_ISSUE are permanent. The skill warns about irreversible operations - always confirm with users before executing these commands.

faq.question: Why do issue creation and update use different assignee fields?
faq.answer: Bitbucket API inconsistency: CREATE_ISSUE uses 'assignee' (username string), while UPDATE_ISSUE uses 'assignee_account_id' (UUID). Always use LIST_WORKSPACE_MEMBERS to get the correct format.
